metadata.title: Kevin Rose from Web 2.0 to Web3
metadata.guest: Kevin Rose from Web 2.0 to Web3

conclusion: Kevin Rose bridges TechTV's Screen Savers era to True Ventures and Modern Finance: Digg peaked at ~38M monthly uniques (ahead of early Facebook), pioneered the Digg button before Facebook's Like, and corrected apocryphal founding lore (Woz lunch myth vs. CmdrTaco on-air inspiration). Post-Digg he angel-invested Twitter, Facebook, and Square; first Bitcoin tweets June 2011; Coinbase shout-out 2012. True Ventures partner with ~10% crypto fund allocation; launches Proof/MoFi and Proof.xyz for NFT-native media. Pattern: true believers in new paradigms capture upside; tourists with business plans arrive late — Andy Warhol/Campbell soup analogy for NFT art. Rated 3/5: strong history and business insight, standard Acquired depth for the era — not a definitive reference vs. dedicated biographies or technical white papers.
background: Special interview spanning Web 2.0 nostalgia (G4/TechTV, Revision3, Diggnation at SXSW with Foo Fighters) through crypto/NFT conviction. Kevin joins from True Ventures after Google Ventures; announces Proof podcast spinout. Ben/David disclose PSL's TrovaTrip carve-out at episode end.
important_facts: Digg reached ~38M monthly uniques at peak — enormous for mid-2000s web and ahead of Facebook's pre-2008 scale; Mark Zuckerberg visited Kevin re: Digg button before Facebook Like.
Kevin's first Bitcoin tweets: June 2011 test wallet; June 2012 Coinbase ('new startup that hosts your Bitcoin wallet') — early consumer-crypto evangelism.
Revision3 (2005) and Diggnation created creator-economy flywheel: media audience fed Digg distribution before 'creator economy' was named.
True Ventures: Kevin as venture partner → full partner; allocates ~10% of fund to crypto — heavy sector concentration for a generalist firm.
Foundation NFT platform used Trent Reznor stems; Kevin met wife via Digg upvotes — CryptoPunk gift story at Twitter suggested-follow party. (2021 episode context).

mental_model.name: Believer Before Investor
mental_model.components: Web 2.0: user-submitted stories (Digg) required faith that crowds improve discovery — skeptics said 'why submit for others?' Web 3: NFT buyers must believe digital scarcity has meaning beyond infinite copy. Founders of paradigm shifts name the category later (Vlad/Webflow 'no-code' after 11 years). Kevin's edge: designer-founder taste (Oak fasting app, watches/Hodinkee) plus investing lens — art + internet + capital. Oak fasting app design sensibility. Hodinkee content-commerce model explicit comparison. Fake ID COMDEX entry as pattern of hustle. Mark visit on Digg button pre-Like documents social graph race.
mental_model.application: When a platform shift arrives, reward participants who use the product for utility before financialization. Kevin's Coinbase 2012 tweet preceded corporate VC crypto mandates by years. Tourists writing business plans on trends underperform community builders 'because the thing needs to exist.'

competitive_advantage: Kevin's moat is cross-era credibility: on-air training from live TechTV (no retakes) plus founder/investor network from Digg's ~38M-user era. Strengths: (1) audience trust from Diggnation/Modern Finance when NFTs were dismissed as infinite-reproducible JPEGs; (2) True's relationship-first capital (Om Malik, long-view partners); (3) design sensibility spotting Beeple and NFT artists early; (4) pattern recognition from missing Uber while J. Cal/Angel invested. Weaknesses: Digg ultimately lost to Facebook/social graph; media fame ≠ durable protocol ownership. NFT/MoFi competes in crowded crypto-media; ~10% crypto allocation at True is cyclical risk. Versus pure VCs, Kevin combines creator brand + checkbook — similar to Ho Nam's public teaching but for consumer crypto culture. G4/TechTV move to LA (Comcast) as culture-clash cautionary tale. Revision3 'netcast' branding predated 'podcast.' Angel misses (Uber to J. Cal) inform True portfolio lens. Foundation.app and Trent Reznor stems show Kevin's artist-investor crossover. Proof.xyz launches NFT-native media; MoFi spinout from Modern Finance. SXSW 2011 Stubb's Diggnation as generational insider moment for Ben — Foo Fighters after-party scale signaled Web 2.0 mainstreaming. Screen Savers live audience training. Digital marketing first job post-TechTV. Google Ventures stint between True roles. Beeple accent and mannerism noted by hosts. CryptoPunk party gift to suggested-follow Twitter employee. 10% True crypto allocation as sector bet sizing case study.

key_insights.view: Digg founding lore was wrong in Wikipedia.
key_insights.question: Was Kevin inspired by lunch with Woz?
key_insights.answer: Business Week cover story claimed Woz lunch triggered Digg; Kevin says CmdrTaco (Slashdot) on Screen Savers and Jay Adelson collaboration were real triggers — guest-on-show → co-founder path, not a single meal epiphany. Episode ties to broader Acquired themes: concentrated ownership, DPI over IRR marketing, and founder quality sensed before metrics confirm. The episode ties numbers to primary sources (Chernow, Russo, Chang interviews, company filings where cited) rather than secondary hype.

key_insights.question: What did Web 2.0 mean to Kevin?
key_insights.answer: Ajax, dynamic user-submitted content, social discovery — practitioners lived it years before the label. Same as NFT artists making art before 'NFT' category existed; deepest participants rarely coin the buzzword.

key_insights.view: Mobile/social were extensions, not new paradigms.
key_insights.question: Why is Web3 different from ML/mobile waves?
key_insights.answer: Instagram=Flickr, WhatsApp=AIM — 1:1 analogues. Web3 builders create because they're in community ('thing needs to exist'), not slide-deck seams. Kevin calls this first true new wave since Digg era.

key_insights.question: Why do early believers win?
key_insights.answer: Parents warned 'don't put personal info on Facebook' — same energy as NFT skeptics. First movers take reputation risk; Kevin's June 2011 Bitcoin tests and Beeple episodes preceded mainstream NFT clicks for Ben.

key_insights.question: What model does Hodinkee prove?
key_insights.answer: Hodinkee enriches purchasable goods (watches) with content — half media, half commerce. Kevin aims Proof/MoFi at NFT-native version: on-chain media, not just MP3 uploads. Watches preview scarce digital goods economics.

top_investment_implications.thesis: Kevin's 2012 Coinbase endorsement illustrates consumer-crypto distribution advantage — monitor whether regulated exchanges retain NFT/wallet mindshare vs. self-custody and specialized platforms like Proof.

golden_quotes: Testing out Bitcoin. Can someone send a couple of cents to this address? — Kevin Rose, first Bitcoin tweet, June 2011.
The people deepest in a thing becoming a thing aren't the people who get to name it. — Ben Gilbert on Web 2.0, no-code, and NFT labels.
Anytime there's a new paradigm… the earliest people… require a leap of faith… there's the most upside. — Kevin on believers vs. tourists.
